name: Scotocercidae
description: Scotocercidae is a monotypic family of passerine bird found in arid regions from North Africa to Central Asia. The single species is well-adapted to desert and semi-desert environments.

children.name: Scotocerca
children.description: A monotypic genus of small warbler inhabiting arid scrub across North Africa and the Middle East.

children.name: Scotocerca inquieta
children.description: The Streaked Scrub Warbler is a small, brownish bird with heavily streaked upperparts and a long, cocked tail. It inhabits arid scrub and rocky wadis, where it gleans insects with restless, acrobatic movements.
